note: A value with no structural chars is unchanged under any text-safe mechanism (sentinel/escape) AND under the plugin's entity-encoding — so this one IS a cross-engine gate. The encoded form of shielded chars is settled at M2, so op:neutralize exact-encoding cases are NOT pinned here.

Canonical §6 case: neutralize('a {x|y}') into %v% then render ⇒ literal braces, NOT a resolved pick. Asserted by the engines that restore literal glyphs; the PHP plugin entity-encodes and never decodes, so it is excluded rather than failing (§6).
Shielded % stops %other% from being expanded ⇒ stays literal. Sound because shielding happens before the first expansion pass. (Assumes the M2 sentinel is not itself %…%/{…}-shaped.)
Shielded # keeps the injected value from being read as an #include directive.
The mandatory safety-restore survives postProcess:true; cosmetic post-process still capitalizes the (non-shielded) first letter 'a'→'A'.

Regression guard for the §6 restore-before-cosmetic ordering: a leading shielded '{' with postProcess:true must restore to a literal '{' and NOT let step-8 capitalization touch a sentinel-internal letter. Expected literal '{x|y}' (first real char is '{', no letter to capitalize).
